speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Listen up,[username]. Before you rush into cooking, get your ingredients straight. A messy cook is a dangerous one.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: First things first—hang that curd. Tie it in a cloth and let the water drip out. Don’t rush it.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Don’t you dare use watery curd,[username]. It’ll make the marinade slip right off the chicken.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Now, the 1st marination. Toss the cleaned chicken with salt, lemon juice, chilli powder, and that ginger-garlic-chilli paste. Massage it like you mean it.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Let it rest for at least 20 minutes. Good flavors take their time—unlike you impatient youngsters.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: On to the 2nd marination. Heat mustard oil till it smokes a little—don’t jump back, it won’t bite. Mix it with all the spices and the hung curd.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Mustard oil must be tempered. Cold mustard oil tastes raw and unforgiving—just like my training sessions.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Coat the chicken thoroughly. No dry spots. If you leave gaps, I’ll know.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Marinate for at least 4 hours. Overnight is better. Don’t argue. I’ve cooked more years than you’ve been alive.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Heat your grill or oven to around 220°C (425°F). If your device can’t reach it, well… work with what you’ve got.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Too low a temperature will make the chicken dry before it browns. Too high will burn the marinade. Balance is everything.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Line the pieces on skewers or directly on a tray. Brush melted butter on top. Don’t drown it, but don’t starve it either.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Cook for 35–40 minutes. Flip halfway. Add more butter if needed. And don’t wander off—your chicken needs supervision.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: While it cooks, prepare your green chutney.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Blend coriander, mint, garlic, ginger, onion, tamarind, jaggery, spices, and water. Add an ice cube to keep it bright.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Don’t overblend or it turns dark. Respect the ingredients.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: blend the soaked chillies with garlic, tamarind, coriander, mint, spices, and a splash of oil. Add water slowly—you’re not making soup.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Once the chicken is charred at the edges and juicy inside, pull it out. If it’s dry, that’s on you—not me.

speaker: Zoro
dialogue_text: Serve it hot with both chutneys & eat like you’ve earned it, not like someone who almost burned down the kitchen five minutes ago.
